brief: Opportunity is heavily concentrated in Brooklyn R7A and R7-2 zones where the 485-x tax exemption paired with Section 8 VPS rents produces high Cash-on-Cash returns between 12.4% and 13.7%. Out of 921 qualifying deals, 289 are city-owned sites that demonstrate massive headroom between estimated land comps ($439/PSF in BK) and maximum walk-away underwriting thresholds. Conversely, Bronx program plays via ELLA/4% LIHTC generate substantial developer fees ($216k-$280k) but show negative land headroom against market sales comps.

targets.play: Execute a 12-unit residential build in Brooklyn utilizing 485-x tax incentives and Section 8 VPS rents to capture a top-tier 13.7% Cash-on-Cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $591,738 against a walk-away cap of $1,588,048 provides $996,310 in valuation headroom.
targets.risk: Site is city-owned, requiring successful navigation of the HPD disposition RFP process rather than a direct seller purchase, alongside R7A zoning verification.

targets.play: Develop 10 units under R7-2 zoning applying 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents to lock in a 12.7% Cash-on-Cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $569,141 versus a walk-away land price of $1,525,802 yields a $956,661 negotiating buffer.
targets.risk: Requires monitoring city disposition timelines through HPD/EDC and conducting thorough physical site condition diligence.

targets.play: Deliver 14 units yielding 12.4% Cash-on-Cash using 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents, with potential to scale across adjacent city-owned lots on Gerry and Bartlett Streets.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $690,612 against a walk-away land cap of $1,760,767 offers $1,070,155 in headroom.
targets.risk: HPD disposition process complexity for single or bundled lot RFPs and verifying zoning bulk limits for R7A.

targets.play: Build 14 units under R6A zoning in Queens leveraging 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents to yield an 11.2% Cash-on-Cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $622,014 against a $1,531,605 walk-away limit provides $909,591 in land price cushion.
targets.risk: City-owned RFP disposition process through HPD/EDC and verifying localized environmental and site conditions.

process_note: Since these initial screening estimates remain pending full diligence and city-owned sites require public HPD/EDC RFP dispositions rather than private acquisitions, our immediate next step is verifying zoning capacity and tracking municipal RFP schedule releases.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
